error_signature: OSError: [Errno 28] No space left on device
title: No space left on device error on DigitalOcean VPS
symptom: The server throws an OSError indicating there is no space left on the device, preventing execution from being stopped.
applies_when: Running n8n on a DigitalOcean VPS
not_this_if: Error occurs on a different hosting provider
root_cause: The error occurs when the disk space on the server is completely utilized, preventing any further operations that require disk access. This can happen due to accumulated logs, unused packages, or insufficient disk allocation.
fix_steps: Run 'sudo rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*' to free up space quickly.
Run 'sudo journalctl --vacuum-time=1d' to clear old system logs.
Increase the disk space on DigitalOcean to the desired size.
Run 'sudo growpart /dev/vda 1' to expand the partition size.
Run 'sudo resize2fs /dev/vda1' to resize the filesystem.
Restart Docker with 'sudo systemctl restart docker'.
Prune Docker data with 'docker system prune -a --volumes'.
Set up a cron job to auto-prune Docker weekly.
agent_instruction: Free up disk space and resize the partition on your DigitalOcean VPS.
safety_notes: Backup your data before performing disk operations.
